mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 4.33% combined with rising CPI (319.775) signals monetary tightening persistence. Sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and increase discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as market reprices growth expectations downward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2008 financial crisis context with elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day windows when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% represents tight labor market. Sustained low unemployment signals wage pressure and inflation persistence, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy stance. This keeps long-term rates elevated as markets price in prolonged monetary tightness, driving 10-year Treasury yields higher.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in September 2008 indicates pre-crisis labor market tightness
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline when UNRATE remains below 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE and 20-day yield changes is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 723,853M) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,366K) show demand weakness. Declining consumer and housing demand signals recession risk, causing flight-to-quality flows into Treasuries and depressing long-term yields as growth expectations collapse over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.5% OR HOUST month-over-month decline > 10%
evidence_refs: fact:October 2008 marks peak of housing crisis with collapsing starts
regime_hint: post-shock recession onset with demand collapse
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of triggered windows despite demand weakness signals, or Rank IC between demand proxies and yield changes < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 103.511) declining signals supply-side contraction and reduced economic capacity. Supply weakness combined with demand destruction creates deflationary pressure, causing real yields to compress and nominal 10-year Treasury yields to fall as terminal rate expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production declining in Q3 2008 amid financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of windows when INDPRO declines, or lead-lag correlation shows INDPRO changes do not precede yield moves by 5-20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 159,218K) combined with rising unemployment signals labor market deterioration. Accelerating job losses trigger recession fears and safe-haven demand for Treasuries, compressing long-term yields downward as investors reprice terminal growth rates lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < -100K AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:September 2008 saw first major employment losses of crisis
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase of financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal (PAYEMS decline + UNRATE rise) shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (R² difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 23,536.293B) growth trajectory combined with collapsing housing and retail demand signals imminent recession. Forward-looking investors reprice terminal growth rates downward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as long-term rate expectations compress over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST + RSAFS combined momentum index falls below 2-quarter moving average by > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2008 GDP growth already slowing; housing/retail collapse signals Q3-Q4 recession
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 60-day windows when demand indicators deteriorate, or 3-month rolling correlation between demand composite and yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 4.33% remains elevated despite emerging financial crisis. Persistent high policy rates combined with credit market dysfunction create liquidity stress. Markets anticipate Fed rate cuts to restore credit flow, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as forward rate expectations shift lower over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND financial stress indicators (TED spread, credit spreads) exceed 2-year 95th percentile
evidence_refs: fact:October 2008 peak financial stress with LIBOR-OIS spreads at crisis highs
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of 5-day windows when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% amid financial stress, or yields fail to decline within 5 trading days of Fed emergency actions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,366K) at multi-year lows signal housing market collapse. Collapsing housing demand destroys wealth, reduces construction employment, and signals broader demand destruction. This triggers deflationary expectations and safe-haven flows into long-duration Treasuries, causing yields to fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400K units (2-year low threshold)
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts collapsed to 1,366K in September 2008, lowest since 2003
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 20-day windows when HOUST is below 1,400K, or Rank IC between HOUST changes and yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
